title: koeleria cristata subsp. glauca
intro: Koeleria cristata subsp. glauca, also known as glaucous June grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is commonly found in mountainous regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Koeleria cristata subsp. glauca include its ability to help lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
